---
title: "Prerequisites"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

::: {.callout-note}
🇫🇷 [Version française](../prerequisites.qmd)
:::

This training assumes you already know how to use the following tools.
It does not teach these languages — it teaches how to **test** them.

---

## Languages and tools

| Tool | Expected level | Relevant module |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| Bash | Write and read simple scripts | 01, 03, 05 |
| Python | Read and modify functions | 02, 03 |
| R | Read and modify functions | 03 |
| Nextflow DSL2 | Understand process, workflow, channel | 04 |
| Git | Commit, branch, diff | all |

---

## Installing the test frameworks

All frameworks are available through the provided conda environment:

```bash
# Install the environment (once only)
bash setup.sh
conda activate ilovetesting
```

What gets installed: `bats-core`, `pytest`, `pytest-cov`, `r-testthat`, `nf-test`.

To install them manually:

| Framework | Command |
|-----------|---------|
| bats | `conda install -c bioconda bats-core` |
| pytest | `pip install pytest pytest-cov` |
| testthat | `install.packages("testthat")` in R |
| nf-test | `conda install -c bioconda nf-test` |

Official documentation:

- bats: <https://bats-core.readthedocs.io>
- pytest: <https://docs.pytest.org>
- testthat: <https://testthat.r-lib.org>
- nf-test: <https://www.nf-test.com>

---

## Apptainer (module 05 only)

Module 05 (container testing) requires Apptainer and runs locally or on HPC.
It is not part of the standard CI.

```bash
apptainer --version   # check availability
```
